library.storageHelp: The mod library shows every installed mod. "Active in game" means the files are in the game folder; "Stored" means the files are kept installed but moved out of the game folder. Modpack checkboxes decide which modpacks include each mod.
library.storeAria: Store {{mod}} (keep installed but move out of the game folder)
library.readOnlyTitle: Followed modpacks are read-only. Duplicate the modpack to edit it.

empty.title: No installed mods

loadOrder.note: Drag rows or use the arrow buttons. This order is stored with the modpack and written to STS2 settings when the modpack is active or activated.
loadOrder.empty: This modpack has no mods to order.
loadOrder.toastSavedApplied: Load order saved for {{name}} and applied to settings.save.

loadOrder.toastSavedSettingsMultiple: Load order saved for {{name}}, but multiple settings.save files were found so none were changed.
loadOrder.toastSavedGameRunning: Load order saved for {{name}}, but STS2 is running. Close the game and activate the modpack to apply it.
loadOrder.toastSavedSettingsFailed: Load order saved for {{name}}, but settings.save could not be updated.

repair.bodyNoOrphans: Re-applies the manifest. No active extra mods to disable — drift will be fixed by toggling state.

autoDetect.title: Auto-detect sources

autoDetect.allLinkedTitle: Nothing to detect — every mod already has a source.
autoDetect.allLinkedBody: All {{count}} installed mod(s) have a GitHub or Nexus link attached, so auto-detect left them alone. To re-link a specific mod manually, open the Mods view, expand the row, and edit its source.
autoDetect.allLinkedBody_one: All {{count}} installed mod has a GitHub or Nexus link attached, so auto-detect left it alone. To re-link a specific mod manually, open the Mods view, expand the row, and edit its source.
autoDetect.allLinkedBody_other: All {{count}} installed mods have a GitHub or Nexus link attached, so auto-detect left them alone. To re-link a specific mod manually, open the Mods view, expand the row, and edit its source.

browseDetail.whatHappensBody: We fetch the latest GitHub release for {{repo}}, download the first asset, and place it in your mods folder. Updates check this repo whenever you run an audit.
browseDetail.nexusInstallBody: Nexus doesn't allow direct downloads — clicking install opens the mod's Files tab in your browser. Click Slow Download (or Manual) — not Mod Manager Download — wait the few seconds, and the manager auto-installs the .zip as soon as it lands in your Downloads folder.

diagnosticBundle.title: Generate support bundle
diagnosticBundle.subtitle: Builds a single text report — game info, active profile, mod list, and recent logs. Nothing is uploaded; the bundle is copied to your clipboard so you can paste it wherever.
diagnosticBundle.readyToast: Bundle ready — scroll the preview to copy manually.
diagnosticBundle.reportGitHubIssueTitle: Bug report from STS2 Mod Manager

logsViewer.emptyLog: Log is empty — actions in the app will appear here.

sourceEditor.title: Sources for {{name}}
sourceEditor.subtitle: Linking enables auto-updates from GitHub releases or Nexus.
sourceEditor.displayNameHint: Optional label shown in the manager only. The manifest name stays unchanged.
sourceEditor.displayDescriptionHint: Optional manager-only description for confusing or untranslated mods.
sourceEditor.notePlaceholder: e.g. downloaded from Patreon, compat patch for v1.8 build
sourceEditor.noteHint: Free-form. Shown on the mod row so you remember where this came from.
sourceEditor.otherLinkHint: For mods that don't live on GitHub or Nexus.
sourceEditor.tagsHint: Optional manager-only categories. Separate tags with commas.
sourceEditor.nexusOnlyMessage: Nexus-only mod — try fetching the GitHub repo from the Nexus description so updates are checked from both sources.

step1.title: Find your Slay the Spire 2 install
step1.subtitle: We'll auto-detect via Steam. You can override anytime in Settings.
step1.pathError: This folder doesn't look like a Slay the Spire 2 install (no {{signature}} found) — pick the install root.

step2.title: What do you want to do?
step2.subtitle: Pick the path that fits — you can always switch later.
step2.playerDesc: Paste a code a friend sent, or browse public modpacks.
step2.creatorDesc: Build a modpack from your installed mods and share it.

playerPath.card1Title: Modpacks are saved sets of mods
playerPath.card1Body: You'll see them on the Modpacks page.
playerPath.card1Detail: Switch between modpacks anytime — your game folder updates to match. Add a friend's code from the Modpacks toolbar to start.
playerPath.card2Body: The Home screen runs the active modpack.
playerPath.card2Detail: When you have an active modpack, the Home screen shows it with a big Play button. That's the whole loop.

creatorPath.card1Detail: The wizard walks you through picking mods from your library and checking they're healthy before you commit. Your installed mods are already there.
creatorPath.card2Body: Sharing is one click — setup happens then, not now.
creatorPath.card2Detail: Sharing a modpack needs a free GitHub account. The app handles that setup when you click Share — there's nothing to configure right now.

publish.publishSubtitle: Uploads your mod files to a GitHub repo on your account so friends can install them.
publish.reshareSubtitle: Same code — followers will see an update prompt next time they open the app.
publish.sharedSubtitle: Anyone with the code can install this exact set of mods.
publish.friendsOnlyDesc: Anyone with the code or link can install it. Not listed on Browse Modpacks.
publish.publicDesc: Listed on Browse Modpacks. Anyone using the app can find and install it.
publish.publicRepoNote: The manager will create a <0>public</0> repo called <1>sts2mm-profiles</1> on your GitHub account (if you don't already have one) to hold your published packs + mod bundles. You can re-publish updates later to the same code; you can also delete or make it private on GitHub at any time.
publish.progressNote: This can take a minute or two for big packs — your mod files are being zipped + uploaded to your <0>sts2mm-profiles</0> repo one at a time. Don't close the window.
publish.copyCodeTitle: Copy the raw share code (paste into the manager's code box)
publish.copyLinkTitle: Copy the install link — clickable in Discord / chat, routes friends through the install bridge page
publish.copyMessageTitle: Copy a paste-ready share message — intro line + install link + raw code in one block
publish.reuseNote: This same code is reused if you re-share later — friends will see updates instead of having to follow a new code.
publish.openRepo: Open my profiles repo on GitHub
publish.failedUploadsToast_one: {{count}} mod failed to upload: {{list}}. Friends installing this code will see "missing mod" for these — try re-publishing.
publish.failedUploadsToast_other: {{count}} mods failed to upload: {{list}}{{more}}. Friends installing this code will see "missing mod" for these — try re-publishing.
publish.failedUploadsHelp: Friends installing this code will see "missing mod" for these - re-publish to retry.

missingBundles.title: Some mods need repair before sharing
missingBundles.body: These mods don't have working bundles. Repair them once and your modpack will share successfully.
missingBundles.openFolderTitle: Open the Mods folder so you can replace the broken files manually

shareImport.shareMessageJoinPack: Join my Slay the Spire 2 modpack "{{packName}}":
shareImport.shareMessagePasteCode: Or paste this code in the manager: {{code}}
shareImport.installConfirmWarning: Modpacks run code (DLLs) inside Slay the Spire 2. Only install from creators you trust.
shareImport.updateDialogCuratorPushed_one: The curator has pushed <1>{{count}}</1> change since you last synced:
shareImport.updateDialogCuratorPushed_other: The curator has pushed <1>{{count}}</1> changes since you last synced:
shareImport.switchDialogActivatePrompt: Activate it now to switch your active modpack? You can switch back to your current saved manifest any time.

shareSetup.title: Set up sharing
shareSetup.explainLine1: To share modpacks with friends, the app saves your modpack list to a small public GitHub repository.
shareSetup.explainLine2: GitHub is free, and the app only needs permission to manage that one repository.
shareSetup.explainLine3: Your friends don't need a GitHub account — they just use the share code or install link.
shareSetup.createTokenLink: Open GitHub to create a token
shareSetup.saveError: That token didn't work. Check it has the right scope and try again.

home.noLaunchTitle: Use the Launch button in the top bar
home.shareThisPackTitle: Publish this pack to a GitHub repo on your account so friends can install it with a code or link
home.clickToCopyShareCode: Click to copy the share code
home.gameNotDetectedDesc: Slay the Spire 2 wasn't found automatically. Open Settings to set the path.
home.shortcutTip: Tip: press {{shortcut}} from anywhere to launch this pack.
home.heroEmptyBody: Modpacks are saved sets of mods. Choose one from your modpacks page, browse public ones, or create your own.

toast.allUpToDate: All modpacks are up to date!
toast.profileNotFoundOnDisk: Couldn't find this profile on disk.
toast.syncedModpack: Synced modpack "{{name}}" - you're up to date!

playerQuickStart.title: Playing modpacks
playerQuickStart.step1: Choose a modpack by pasting a friend's code on Home, browsing public modpacks, or creating your own.
playerQuickStart.step2: When you've added a modpack, switch to it from Home or the topbar so it becomes your active modpack.
playerQuickStart.step3: Click Launch to start STS2 with the mods from your active modpack loaded.
playerQuickStart.step4: Switch modpacks anytime — the app updates your game folder so you don't have to manage files manually.

creatorQuickStart.title: Making modpacks
creatorQuickStart.step1: Open Create Modpack and choose how to start: from your active mods, empty, or by cloning an existing modpack.
creatorQuickStart.step2: Pick which installed mods belong to this modpack. You can change membership later.
creatorQuickStart.step3: Check the health summary so you know how many mods have linked sources and which ones have available updates.
creatorQuickStart.step4: When you're ready to share, click Share. The app walks you through a one-time GitHub setup so friends can install your modpack from a short code.
creatorQuickStart.step5: Maintain your modpack later by running Mod Health, accepting updates, or freezing mods at a known good version.

modpackWhat.q: What is a modpack and why do I need one?
modpackWhat.a: A modpack is a saved set of mods plus a load order. The mod manager applies the modpack to your game folder so you don't have to install or enable mods one by one. Modpacks make it easy to switch between curated experiences or share your setup with friends.

storedMeaning.q: What does 'Stored' mean for a mod?
storedMeaning.a: Stored means the mod is installed on your computer but isn't currently in the game folder. Stored mods stay available — you can move them back into the active game folder anytime by toggling them on, or include them in a modpack.

githubWhy.q: Why do I need GitHub to share a modpack?
githubWhy.a: To share a modpack, the app needs somewhere to host the modpack's information and any bundled mod files. The app uses a small public GitHub repository that it creates and manages for you. GitHub is free, the app only needs permission to manage that one repository, and your friends never need a GitHub account — they just use the share code.

blockedUpdate.q: Why is this mod update blocked?
blockedUpdate.a: A mod update can be blocked because it requires a newer version of STS2 than the one you have installed. Often the mod author releases the new version when the STS2 beta branch is updated. Switching STS2 to the matching beta branch on Steam usually unblocks the update.

freeze.a: Freezing a mod locks it at its current version. The mod manager won't update or change the mod until you unfreeze it. Use Freeze when a mod is working and you want to keep it stable while other mods update.

skipUpdate.a: Skip hides one specific update — usually one that's known to be broken or that you're not ready for. The mod stays at its current version, and the skipped update stops showing up. When a newer release appears later, you'll see that one as a normal update.

nexusManual.q: Why must I download some mods from Nexus manually?
nexusManual.a: Nexus Mods requires you to download some mods manually through your web browser. The mod manager opens the right page for you and watches your Downloads folder, so when the file finishes downloading the app automatically installs it into the correct mod.

publishedSubset.q: Why isn't every installed mod in my published modpack?
publishedSubset.a: A published modpack only includes the mods you marked as part of that modpack. Mods that are installed but not in the modpack stay on your computer as 'stored' and are not sent to friends. This keeps shared modpacks small and avoids accidentally publishing your private mods.

settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Configure game paths, accounts, and preferences

general.couldNotVerify: Couldn't verify {{file}} in this folder. Pick the install root, not a subfolder.
general.autoDetectHint: Click Auto-detect to find your Steam install, or Browse to pick the folder manually.
general.couldNotAutoDetect: Could not auto-detect game path. Please set it manually.
general.launchDesc: How the Launch button and Ctrl/⌘ L start Slay the Spire 2.
general.steamDesc: Launches via Steam. Required for cloud saves, achievements, and Proton on Linux.
general.directDesc: Skips the Steam launcher and runs the game executable. Steam itself still needs to be running — STS2 uses Steamworks for saves and achievements. Useful for Family Sharing borrowers (the lender's library lock blocks normal Steam launches) and Steam offline mode. Not supported for Proton/Linux installs.

accounts.nexusSavedHelp: Saved · Nexus mods will appear in Browse.
accounts.nexusApiLink: Get your API key from Nexus Mods
accounts.nexusTierNote: Free-tier only. To install a Nexus mod, click the "Slow Download" / "Manual" button on Nexus — the app catches the zip from your Downloads folder. The "Mod Manager Download" button isn't wired through. Nexus Premium's instant-download API isn't either, so paid subscribers don't get faster downloads here.
accounts.githubSavedHelp: Saved · raises API rate limit to 5,000 req/hr.
accounts.githubHelp: Optional for browsing GitHub mods (raises rate limit to 5,000 req/hr from 60/hr without auth). Required if you want to publish modpacks.
accounts.githubClassicPat: Classic PAT — check the repo scope.
accounts.githubFinePat: Fine-grained PAT — repository access scoped to sts2mm-profiles (or "All repositories"), with Contents: Read and write + Administration: Read and write (Administration is only needed for the one-time repo create; you can drop it after).

backups.title: Backups
backups.desc: Auto-saved before every launch and Vanilla Mode - keeps the last 5
backups.noBackupsHint: A backup is auto-created before every launch. You can also create one manually.
backups.restoreConfirmBody: This will replace your current mods with the snapshot. Your current state is not saved unless you back it up first.
backups.restoreCheckbox: Save current as a new backup before restoring
backups.deleteConfirmBody: You won't be able to restore it after this.

audit.title: Mod audit
audit.desc: Compare each installed mod against its source · freeze to keep the current version and on/off state
audit.noAuditHint: Run an audit to see which mods have updates available, which are frozen, and which can't be matched against a source.
audit.goneTitle: GitHub releases exist but have no installable assets — auto-update can't fetch from this source
audit.snoozedTitle: This update is skipped until a newer version appears.
audit.skipUpdateTooltip: Hide v{{version}} until a newer version appears.
audit.showUpdateAgainTooltip: Clear the skip and show this update again.
audit.snoozedToast: Skipped updates for '{{name}}' until the next release.
audit.updateBlockedTitle: The newest release requires a different STS2 game version.
audit.pinTooltip: Freeze — keep this exact version and on/off state. Updates and modpack/profile changes will skip it.
audit.unpinTooltip: Unfreeze — allow updates and profile changes again

settings.onLatestRelease: On the source's latest installable release.
settings.updateGameBlocked: Latest {{version}} requires game {{required}}. Will install the newest compatible release: {{compatibleVersion}}.
settings.unpinnedToast: Unfrozen '{{name}}' — updates will be checked again.
settings.pinnedToast: Frozen '{{name}}' — updates and profile changes will skip it.
settings.noAssetTooltip: GitHub release {{version}} ships no installable asset. Auto-update can't fetch from this source.
settings.auditGameVersionBlock: This mod's manifest declares min_game_version={{declared}}. Your STS2 install reports {{installed}}. Until you update STS2 (or switch beta branches), the game's loader will silently skip this mod.
settings.auditWontLoad: Won't load on your game (needs Slay the Spire 2 ≥ v{{version}}; you have v{{installed}}). Use Repair on the Mods row to roll back to a compatible release.
settings.auditCompatTag: Latest release {{latest}} requires game {{required}}. Update will walk back to {{compatible}}, the newest release compatible with your STS2 build.
settings.auditCompatTagVisible: Latest {{latest}} needs game {{required}}; Update will install {{compatible}} (newest compatible).
settings.auditAlreadyCompat: Latest release {{version}} requires game {{required}}. You're already on {{installed}}, the newest release that runs on your current STS2 build. Update STS2 (or switch beta branches) to pick up the newer mod release.
settings.auditAlreadyCompatVisible: Latest {{version}} needs game {{required}}; you're on {{installed}} - the newest version that runs on your game.

mods.repairConfirmBody: This will delete the current on-disk install and re-download the latest release from the linked GitHub source. Use it when a mod is broken (version reads "unknown", game won't load it, etc.). Make sure STS2 is closed.
mods.deleteConfirmBody: This permanently removes the mod files from disk. This cannot be undone.
mods.deleteAllConfirmBody: Every mod folder will be permanently removed from disk. This cannot be undone.
mods.deleteAllConfirmWarning: Profiles that include these mods will be unable to launch until mods are reinstalled.
mods.pinnedTitle: Frozen — updates and modpack/profile changes will skip this mod's version and enabled/disabled state
mods.latestTitle: On the source's latest installable release.
mods.updateClickTitle: Click to update from v{{current}} → v{{target}}. Same pipeline as Settings → Audit.
mods.pinDesc: Don't update, replace, enable, or disable this mod until you unfreeze it. Use when beta updates or modpacks would break your setup.
mods.unpinDesc: Allow updates, replacements, and profile/modpack changes again.
mods.repairDesc: Force-reinstall from GitHub. Use when version reads "unknown", the game refuses to load this mod, or the install otherwise looks broken.
mods.repairNeedSource: Link a GitHub source first via "Edit sources…" — repair fetches from GitHub.
mods.subtitle: {{total}} installed · {{enabled}} active{{disabled}}
mods.checking: Checking each mod against its source…
mods.checkForUpdates: Check each mod against its source for updates.
mods.allUpToDate: Every linked mod is on its source's latest installable release.
mods.updateAllTitle: Update every GitHub-linked mod with a pending update. Frozen mods are skipped.
mods.quickAddLabel: GitHub URL, Nexus URL, or github:owner/repo
mods.emptyDescription: Browse to install a mod, paste a friend's profile code, or drop a .zip anywhere on this window.
mods.disambiguatorTitle: Two installed mods are named "{{name}}". This one is identified by {{identifier}}.
mods.nexusUpdateTitle: Nexus has v{{nexusVer}} (you have v{{localVer}}). Click to open the Nexus Files tab — pick "Slow Download" / "Manual" and the manager will auto-install the zip when it lands in Downloads.
mods.snoozedTitle: Update suggestion snoozed at v{{version}}. Auto-expires when a newer release appears.
mods.gameVersionBlockedTitle: Newer release exists (v{{target}}) but it requires a newer Slay the Spire 2 build. Update STS2 (or switch beta branches) to pick it up; the manager can install an older compatible release in the meantime.
mods.minGameVersionTitle: This mod's manifest declares min_game_version={{minVer}}. Your STS2 is v{{yourVer}}. The game's loader will silently skip this mod until you update STS2 or switch beta branches. Use Repair (advanced kebab) to roll back to a compatible release.
mods.unsnoozeDesc: Show the update suggestion again for the current upstream release.
mods.snoozeDesc: Skip this update until a newer release appears upstream (currently v{{version}}). Use this when the website's announced version doesn't match the file's actual version.

toast.pinned: Frozen {{name}} — updates and profile changes will skip it
toast.repairNoGithub: '{{name}}' has no GitHub source linked — repair fetches from GitHub. Add a source via Edit sources… first.
toast.snoozed: Skipped updates for {{name}} until next release
toast.noGitHubInNexus: No GitHub link found in Nexus description for {{name}}
toast.foundNexusMod: Found Nexus mod: {{name}}. Open it on Nexus and click "Slow Download" / "Manual" — the app will catch the zip from your Downloads folder.

mods.allInstalledSubtitle: Every mod installed on your computer. Your active modpack decides which ones load in the game.

sort.label: Sort
sort.visualOnly: Visual only: this sort does not change load order.

mods.rollbackNoSource: '{{name}}' has no GitHub source linked — rollback fetches from GitHub. Add a source via Edit sources… first.
mods.rollbackConfirmBody: This will delete the current on-disk install and recover a compatible GitHub release. If this install still has a usable version, rollback picks the closest lower release; if the version reads "unknown", it reinstalls the last known-good release. Use it when the newest release is broken. Make sure STS2 is closed.
mods.rollbackConfirmWarning: Rollback is a beta recovery feature. It preserves mod configs and resolves a compatible GitHub release before deleting files, but some mods package releases inconsistently, so check the result before launching a long run.
mods.rollbackDesc: Recover a compatible GitHub release. Uses the previous release when the current version is known, or the last known-good release when this install reads "unknown".
mods.rollbackNeedSource: Link a GitHub source first via "Edit sources..." - rollback fetches from GitHub.

kebab.activateDesc: Move this mod's files back into the game folder so STS2 loads it.
kebab.disableDesc: Move this mod's files out of the game folder. The mod stays installed but won't load until you activate it again.
